name: Dahi (Set Yogurt)
tagline: Homemade set curd — calcium, B12, and live probiotics

hero.alt: Snow-white set yogurt in a clay matka with a thick layer of malai on top
hero.caption: dahi — set in a clay matka overnight

steps.title: Boil and cool the milk
steps.detail: Bring milk to a rolling boil, then let it simmer for 5 minutes (this kills any wild bacteria and concentrates the milk). Cool to lukewarm — about 42°C, or warm enough that your finger can stay in for 5 seconds without discomfort.
steps.tip: Hotter than 45°C kills the starter; colder than 38°C and it won't set.

steps.title: Add the starter
steps.detail: Whisk the starter yogurt with 2 tbsp of the warm milk in a small bowl until smooth, then stir back into the rest of the milk. Pour into your final setting vessel — earthen matka or steel bowl.
steps.tip: A clay matka draws moisture out and gives a thicker, more traditional set.

steps.title: Set in a warm spot
steps.detail: Cover and leave undisturbed in a warm corner (oven with light on, or kitchen counter in summer) for 6–8 hours. When it's set, refrigerate at least 2 hours before serving.
steps.tip: Don't shake it during setting — it'll break the curds.

healthFacts: Live cultures (Lactobacillus, Streptococcus thermophilus) support gut microbiota.
200g serving delivers ~250mg calcium, 1.5µg B12, and 0.4mg riboflavin.
Fermented dairy is better tolerated than fresh milk in mild lactose intolerance.
Iodine from dairy supports thyroid function (TSH-relevant).
